mechanism_narrative: An increase in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) signals stronger consumer demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return correlation with month-over-month RSAFS change is negative or insignificant (p>0.10) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate (above 5%) indicates restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate on future cash flows and slows economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, the average 60-day forward return is not statistically different from zero or positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with elevated interest rates signals a late-cycle overheating economy where wage pressures and borrowing costs squeeze corporate margins, resulting in negative stock returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS level in a linear regression predicting 20-day returns does not have a negative coefficient significant at the 5% level over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the early-cycle recovery phase after an economic trough, rising industrial production indicates robust demand and capacity utilization, which boosts investor confidence and drives positive long-term equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods identified as early-cycle (e.g., first two years after NBER trough), the correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is non-positive or insignificant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts capture real estate activity and consumer sentiment; rising starts signal economic momentum and shift equity valuations upward within days of the release.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following a higher-than-previous-month HOUST release does not average more than zero (one-sided test p>0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, leading to negative stock market returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5%, the 20-day forward return is not negative on average (one-sided test p>0.10) or the signal has no predictive power in a regime classification model.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth accompanied by a high federal funds rate indicates that consumption is being sustained by credit and is unsustainable, foreshadowing a slowdown that depresses equity prices over the quarter.
failure_condition: The product of RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS level (or a similar interaction) fails to predict negative 60-day returns at a significance level of 5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions, sustained increases in payroll employment reinforce the expansion narrative and raise earnings expectations, contributing to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During NBER-dated expansions, the month-over-month change in PAYEMS does not positively predict 20-day forward returns with a significant coefficient (p<0.05).
